title: The canonical KdV update preserves constant states

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant state, the canonical KdV conservative update returns the same constant at every grid index.
assumptions: all scalar parameters are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n and i are natural-number grid or range indices as used in the theorem statement
the KdV scaffold definitions are those in LeanNumerics.FiniteDifference.Kdv
informal.expected_conclusion: Constant states are preserved by the canonical KdV update.

declarations.name: kdvCanonicalUpdate_const_state
declarations.statement: theorem kdvCanonicalUpdate_const_state (scale c : Real) (i : Nat) : kdvCanonicalUpdate scale (fun _ => c) i = c

tasks.input: Translate this KdV conservative scaffold theorem into Lean 4: For a constant state, the canonical KdV conservative update returns the same constant at every grid index.
tasks.target: theorem kdvCanonicalUpdate_const_state (scale c : Real) (i : Nat) : kdvCanonicalUpdate scale (fun _ => c) i = c

tasks.input: Given the KdV scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by reusing kdvConservativeUpdate_const_state at coefficients 3 and 1.
